measurements.claim: The sixteenth-wave package adds a bounded Lean cyclic summation-by-parts replay rung while keeping smooth periodic integration by parts NOT_REPLAYED and the parent Millennium problem UNVERIFIABLE.
evidence: the package describes one kernel-checked cyclic summation-by-parts replay rung
the package states smooth periodic integration by parts is not replayed
the package states the parent Navier-Stokes Millennium problem remains UNVERIFIABLE

measurements.claim: The sixteenth-wave theorem is stored in a separate Lean file and preserves the fourteenth-wave and fifteenth-wave source hashes.
evidence: lean-cyclic-summation-by-parts replay receipt records both prior source hashes as preserved

measurements.claim: CyclicSummationByPartsPreflight.lean compiles with Lean exit code 0 and replays lastPair, gradientOpen, divergenceInterior, cyclicGradientSum, cyclicDivergenceSum, open_summation_by_parts, and cyclic_summation_by_parts_cancels.
The sixteenth-wave source refresh retains 13 arXiv metadata rows, 13 unique IDs, verifies both Gather stores as MATCH, and demotes the rows to SOURCE_LEAD_ONLY.
The website copy and official-copy scaffold distinguish CYCLIC_SUMMATION_BY_PARTS_MATCH from NOT_REPLAYED smooth theorem claims.
evidence: research-formal-replay-preflight.html states CYCLIC_SUMMATION_BY_PARTS_MATCH for the finite paired-stencil theorem
research-formal-replay-preflight.html states continuous periodic integration by parts is NOT_REPLAYED

measurements.claim: The sixteenth-wave content queue includes explicit do-not-post boundaries for Navier-Stokes, smooth periodic integration by parts, source truth, exhaustive coverage, native BuildLang relation receipts, and accepted-publication claims.
evidence: Do Not Post forbids Project Telos solved Navier-Stokes
Do Not Post forbids Lean proved smooth periodic integration by parts
Do Not Post forbids arXiv metadata proves paper truth
Do Not Post forbids latest/exhaustive coverage
Do Not Post forbids native BuildLang relation-invariant support
Do Not Post forbids website copy is submitted, accepted, or peer-reviewed
